summary: Strong first set; Days Between & Brokedown Palace shine despite uneven second set
blurb: The show opens with a tight first set featuring Bertha, Peggy‑O and a bright Lazy River Road. Highlights include Jerry’s soaring “Days Between,” Phil’s vocal peak on “Unbroken Chain,” and an emotional “Brokedown Palace” encore. The second set falters around “Victim Or The Crime,” but the rhythm section holds together with a lengthy drum groove.
review: June 4, 1995 at Shoreline Amphitheatre delivered a mixed bag of classic Dead magic. The first set rolled on strong – Bertha and the swing‑in from Wang Dang Doodle into Peggy‑O felt fresh, and Lazy River Road and Cassidy kept the energy humming. The real high points came later: Jerry’s vocal performance on “Days Between” was soulful and his guitar sang out beautifully, while Phil Lesh stole the show with a powerful delivery of “Unbroken Chain.” The second set, however, stumbled; “Victim Or The Crime” veered into the wrong key and felt chaotic, yet the band salvaged it with an expansive drum segment that stretched close to half an hour. The night closed on a heartfelt note as the band slipped into a moving “Brokedown Palace,” giving the audience a poignant farewell feeling. Of the available tapes, the soundboard capture (gd95-06-04.sbd.13929.sbeok.shnf) offers the clearest listen – good SBD fidelity with only mild hiss and a slight tape speed quirk – making it the recommended source for anyone wanting to experience the highs while tolerating the occasional slip.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise specific moments (Days Between, Brokedown Palace, Unbroken Chain) and note strong vocal performances, while also flagging sloppy second‑set playing and Jerry’s waning guitar work. The mix of positive highlights across several sources outweighs the negatives but does not reach a uniformly glowing consensus, yielding a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard source with good overall fidelity, capturing the standout songs while minimizing tape hiss and speed issues.

key_highlights: Jerry’s soaring vocal and guitar on “Days Between”
Phil’s powerful vocal delivery on “Unbroken Chain”
A lengthy, high‑energy drum segment that holds the show together despite a shaky second set

band_performance.Jerry: His playing was uneven – moments of waning guitar and occasional sloppiness – but he delivered a soulful vocal on “Days Between” and a heartfelt solo that lifted the show.
band_performance.Phil: Slipped into the wrong key on “Victim Or The Crime,” yet his bass work stayed solid and his vocals shone on “Unbroken Chain.”
band_performance.Mickey: Joined Bill in an unusually long drum groove (~30 min) that kept the rhythm tight through a faltering second set.
band_performance.Billy: Provided steady drumming alongside Mickey, helping to anchor the band during extended improvisational sections.
